topic: Reform UK MP count and defection timeline
summary: Reform UK has 8 MPs as of late Jan 2026. 5 won July 2024 election + Kruger (Sep 2025) + Jenrick (Jan 15) + Rosindell (Jan 18) + Braverman (Jan 26). May 7 deadline set for defections.
content:
Current 8 Reform UK MPs:
Original 5 from July 2024 election: Farage, Tice, Anderson, Lowe/McMurdock, plus one other
Defectors from Conservatives:
- Danny Kruger: Sep 2025
- Robert Jenrick: Jan 15, 2026
- Andrew Rosindell: Jan 18, 2026
- Suella Braverman: Jan 26, 2026

Key context:
- Reform leads polls by 11pts over Labour, 14pts over Conservatives
- MRP forecasts Reform winning 381 seats at next election
- Conservatives projected to fall to ~45-70 MPs
- 22 former (non-sitting) Conservative MPs have also defected
- Farage set May 7 deadline for defections
- Jacob Rees-Mogg called for by-elections for defectors but is NOT a sitting MP himself
- No new defections reported since Braverman (Jan 26) - ~2 week pause
- Defection burst: 3 sitting MPs in 11 days in January
